element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Overhead view city-building; player learns arrow shooting and miracle selection (rain, lightning, sunlight, wind, earthquakes) to guide civilization growth
Side-scrolling platformer; basic jumping and enemy defeat introduced before boss encounter
Four lairs per region continuously spawn flying monsters; player must seal lairs to advance civilization level and unlock advanced structures
One boss per action stage; bosses based on mythology (manticore shown as example); player must defeat to progress
Master gains HP and SP increases by growing total population; creates feedback loop between simulation and action performance
Higher civilization levels unlock better homes and structures; requires lair sealing progression
Defeating lieutenants recovers Master's powers through restored belief; narrative progression tied to boss defeats
Action-only sequences with multiple boss encounters culminating in final Tanzra fight; no simulation break between fights

typical_relief_triggers: Completing action stage and defeating boss
Sealing monster lairs to reduce spawning threat
Building population and unlocking new structures
Master leveling up and gaining HP/SP
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounter at end of action sequence
Continuous monster spawning from active lairs
Platform hazards and enemy attacks in action stages
Multiple consecutive boss fights in final level
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dual-rhythm structure: safe city-building simulation alternates with dangerous platforming action. Each level cycles simulation → action → boss, creating predictable tension peaks. Final level breaks pattern with continuous boss marathon, sustaining danger without relief cycle.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Action stages begin with basic platforming and enemy patterns; simulation mode introduces miracle system gradually; player builds Master power through population growth
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monster lair sealing becomes strategic requirement; boss encounters introduce mythology-based attack patterns; platforming complexity increases with stage hazards
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multiple lieutenant bosses introduce region-specific challenges; final level removes simulation breaks, forcing player to tackle boss marathon with accumulated power from all prior levels
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (introduces danger rhythm after safe simulation)
Final level structure (eliminates simulation relief cycle; continuous action challenge)
Tanzra final boss (climax after six lieutenant defeats)

notable_level_patterns.description: Simulation phase (lair sealing, population growth) → Action stage 1 (platforming, enemies) → Action stage 2 (platforming, boss fight). Master power increases through population, directly affecting next level's action performance.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Bloodpool region with manticore boss example)

notable_level_patterns.description: Overhead city-building simulation controls pacing and resource management (miracles, lair sealing). Side-scrolling action stages provide skill-based challenges tied to narrative (defeating lieutenants). Belief mechanic (population worship) gates Master's power recovery.
Final level breaks standard cycle: action-only sequences without simulation breaks between encounters. Player faces multiple bosses in succession, testing accumulated skills and power.

Four monster lairs per region continuously spawn enemies. Sealing them requires civilization advancement. Higher civilization levels unlock advanced structures and allow population to seal lairs, creating positive feedback during simulation phases.
